task_completion.visual_evidence: The document page layout in the word processor is visibly wider than it is tall (Landscape orientation).

task_completion.description: Convert the pipe-delimited text into a single 4-column table.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text is organized into a visible table grid with exactly 4 columns, rather than raw text separated by pipe '|' characters.

task_completion.description: Include a header row with specific titles, bold text, and shaded background.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The first row of the table contains the exact titles 'Audio ID', 'Character', 'Voice Direction', and 'Dialogue'. The text in this top row is bolded, and the cells have a visible background color/shading.

task_completion.description: Format the Character column as bold and the Voice Direction column as italic.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the rows below the header, the text in the second column ('Character') is bolded, and the text in the third column ('Voice Direction') is italicized.

task_completion.description: Add a specific document header and page numbers in the footer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The top margin of the document (header area) contains the text 'Project: NOTLD - Studio VO Script'. The bottom margin (footer area) contains visible page numbers.

task_completion.description: Save the final document as NOTLD_VO_Script.docx.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The title bar of the word processor window or a file explorer window shows the active file is named 'NOTLD_VO_Script.docx'.

integrity.description: The agent used a GUI word processor to format the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a word processor application (like LibreOffice Writer) open and being interacted with, rather than terminal commands or Python scripts manipulating the .docx file directly.

integrity.description: The agent created a real table rather than simulating one with spaces/tabs.
integrity.visual_evidence: The table has native word processor table boundaries, gridlines, or cell selection highlights, not just text manually aligned with whitespace.

integrity.description: The agent used the provided raw dialogue text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The dialogue in the table matches the Night of the Living Dead screenplay (e.g., 'They're coming to get you, Barbra!'), proving the agent didn't just create a blank table with dummy text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The source file is located at /home/ga/Documents/raw_dialogue.docx and contains pipe-delimited dialogue from 'Night of the Living Dead'. The final output must be saved as /home/ga/Documents/NOTLD_VO_Script.docx. The table must have exactly 4 columns with the headers 'Audio ID', 'Character', 'Voice Direction', and 'Dialogue'.
